title: Collection of Mammalian Embryonic Palate Tissue
author: Matthew Fox
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5tag6ie

steps.title: Euthanize pregnant mouse via primary and...
steps.instruction: Euthanize pregnant mouse via primary and secondary modes of IACUC approved euthanasia CO2 gas at 2L/min until 1 minute after last gasp. Cervical separation

steps.title: Using scissors, create an abdominal incision...
steps.instruction: Using scissors, create an abdominal incision from the genitalia to the rib cage.

steps.title: Locate and lift both strands of...
steps.instruction: Locate and lift both strands of embryos so they are clear of all organs.

steps.title: Free embryos by cutting the tissues...
steps.instruction: Free embryos by cutting the tissues connecting anteriorly at the kidney and posterior at the base of the uterine horn.

steps.title: Transfer the embryos to the 50ml...
steps.instruction: Transfer the embryos to the 50ml tube containing PBS on ice. Embryos should remain on ice until all sample collection is complete.

steps.title: Place adult mouse in ziplock bag...
steps.instruction: Place adult mouse in ziplock bag and place in Animal Room freezer

steps.title: In a dish containing PBS, remove...
steps.instruction: In a dish containing PBS, remove each embryo from the uterine strand using micro scissors.

steps.title: Cut open the amniotic sacs and...
steps.instruction: Cut open the amniotic sacs and cut the umbilical cords to free the embryos from the placenta.

steps.title: Remove the heads of the embryos...
steps.instruction: Remove the heads of the embryos and transfer to a dish of clean PBS.

steps.title: Transfer an embryo head to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer an embryo head to a clean sorting dish and place under stereoscope at 1.75X zoom.

steps.title: Excise palatal shelves via cutting the...
steps.instruction: Excise palatal shelves via cutting the tissues connecting the palatal shelves to the nasal palatal and maxillary processes.

steps.title: Transfer palate tissue to 1
steps.instruction: Transfer palate tissue to 1.5ml centrifuge tube on ice.

steps.title: Preserve palate tissue by freezing at...
steps.instruction: Preserve palate tissue by freezing at -80 °C in PBS solution.
